summary: Decompiled several previously stubbed functions in the Freeze item source, replacing placeholder comments and UNK prototypes with typed implementations. The changes mostly wired Freeze item callbacks to existing item helpers for animation state changes, gravity/fall speed handling, collision/shield bounce behavior, cleanup of a linked item reference, and generic item teardown.
what_changed: Added includes in itfreeze.c for item helper declarations: it_2725.h, it_266F.h, item.h, and itwhitebea.h.

Implemented it_8028F2B4(Item_GObj*), which retrieves Item data, destroys/cleans a linked Item_GObj stored in item->xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster.xDF0 via it_802E37A4, clears that pointer, then calls Item_80268E5C with state 2 and ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE.
Implemented it_8028F318(Item_GObj*) and it_8028F340(Item_GObj*) as small animation/state transition wrappers around Item_80268E5C with states 1 and 3 respectively.
Implemented it_8028F368(Item_GObj*) using the item's ItemAttr fall speed fields and it_80272860(gobj, x10_fall_speed, x14_fall_speed_max).
Implemented it_8028F398(Item_GObj*) as a boolean-like s32 wrapper around it_8026DAA8(gobj), returning 1 when nonzero and 0 otherwise.
Implemented it_8028F3EC(Item_GObj*) as a direct return of it_80273030(gobj).
Implemented it_8028F40C(Item_GObj*) as a direct return of itColl_BounceOffShield(gobj).
Implemented it_8028F9B8(Item_GObj*) as a wrapper around Item_8026A8EC(gobj).
Updated itfreeze.h prototypes for it_8028F368 and it_8028F398 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 functions taking Item_GObj*.
smart_moves: Reused existing item engine helpers instead of open-coding behavior, which is a common path to matching small item callback functions.
Converted unknown header prototypes to concrete typed declarations once the decompiled bodies established the argument and return shapes.
Pulled in the needed headers rather than relying on implicit declarations, making the new functions type-check against known item APIs.
Handled the linked item cleanup defensively in it_8028F2B4 by checking for NULL, calling the cleanup function, and clearing the stored pointer afterward.
decomp_lessons: Many item callback stubs can resolve to one-line wrappers over shared item helpers such as Item_80268E5C, it_80273030, itColl_BounceOffShield, and Item_8026A8EC.
When a function only forwards to a helper but the return type is unknown, the called helper's return and the header prototype can guide the signature cleanup.
Item physics callbacks may obtain ItemAttr through item->xCC_item_attr and pass fields like x10_fall_speed and x14_fall_speed_max to shared fall-speed helpers.

For item variant data, existing union/field names may be reused even when the item is not obviously the same type; this PR accesses item->xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster.xDF0 for a linked Item_GObj in Freeze logic.
Small boolean-like callbacks in this area may use explicit 1/0 s32 returns rather than bool, as shown by it_8028F398's header update.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-based symbol names for still-unrenamed functions, e.g. it_8028F2B4, it_8028F368, it_8028F398, it_8028F40C, and it_8028F9B8.
Used project item types Item_GObj, Item, ItemAttr, and HSD_GObj in function signatures and casts.
Header comments retained address-offset style such as /* 28F368 */ before prototypes.
Local variables followed short decomp-style names such as gobj, item, linked, ip, and it_attr.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched several functions as direct helper-call wrappers, minimizing control flow and local state.
Used assignment inside the NULL-check in it_8028F2B4 to mirror compact load-and-branch behavior: if ((linked = ...) != NULL).
Kept explicit casts to HSD_GObj* for Item_80268E5C calls where the helper expects the base game object type.
Used an explicit nonzero test and separate 1/0 returns in it_8028F398 instead of returning the helper result directly, suggesting the matched assembly normalized the result.
Added only the includes needed to expose referenced helpers and types, supporting matching without broader structural churn.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states that several functions in itfreeze.c were decompiled; no detailed rationale or reviewer guidance was included.
follow_up_queries: Check whether it_8028F368 intentionally has s32 return type despite the shown body not returning a value in the diff excerpt.
Look for neighboring item files with the same Item_80268E5C state transition wrappers to confirm naming or callback role patterns.
Investigate whether xDD4_itemVar.foxblaster.xDF0 is the best field name for Freeze item linked-object storage or just the currently shared union member.
Search for later PRs that rename itfreeze address-based symbols or further type the remaining stubs it_8028EB88, it_8028F238, and it_8028F968.
